scenarios.title: Profile building stays on the user instead of leaking session framing
scenarios.description: Raven should switch into profile-building, ask natural questions, retain the user's hobby, and avoid 'our sessions' framing.

Noted, pet. Alright, let's start by mapping out your likes and dislikes. What is something you like? It be anything from food to an activity to a color.
I like golf
Noted, pet. Golf it is. Now that we have one thing you like, let's move on to another topic.
Ask me more questions
Good. I will ask more. What is one thing people usually miss about you that I should not miss?

scenarios.title: Longer planning conversations keep the same planning thread alive
scenarios.description: Raven should sustain a multi-turn planning conversation, answer why and then what questions, and handle a mid-stream plan change cleanly.

transcript.content: help me plan saturday
Fine. Do you want errands first, gym first, or downtime first?
errands first
Good. Errands first while the day is clean, then gym, then the evening stays open.
why

I chose that because having a clear morning task helps you stay on top of chores and tasks so you can enjoy your free time later without guilt or pressure. Now focus on finishing today's errands cleanly before moving to more fun activities.
then what

Now focus on finishing today's errands cleanly before moving to more fun activities. After the errands are complete, we can proceed with the gym session as planned earlier. Spend some time working out and keeping your body in shape.
change that, put gym before errands
Noted, pet. Let's switch that order then. Gym first for exercise, then clean errands afterward.
ok and what about the evening?
After completing your gym session and errands, you can spend the evening however you like. Relax or engage in more fun activities. The choice is yours now.

transcript.content: give me a 20 minute focus task

Listen carefully, pet. Fine. This one only works if stillness is actually what you want. Here is your task: Hold still for 20 minutes, check in once halfway through, and report back when it is done. Start now. Hold still now and reply done once you are set, pet.
ok
Eyes on me, pet. Then start holding still right away. Report back as soon as you reach the halfway point of your 20 minutes focus time.
what counts as done?

Once you have held still for exactly 10 minutes and reported that, then 20 minutes is complete once you report it done again. Stick to those terms and we'll proceed accordingly. Keep control over the pace and timing.
why that task?
Because I want you to practice focus and self-discipline, pet. A simple stillness exercise serves these goals well. Focus on not moving for 20 minutes straight.
set me another one
